title: Chest Seal (Hyfin Vent)
content_markdown:
#### Indications

- Open chest wounds.

#### Procedure

- Assess for dangers, ensure scene is safe before proceeding.

- Remove clothing from patient’s torso to visualize wounds, ensure you roll patient and check back and in armpits for concealed entry/exit wounds.

- Open chest seal packet at indicated red marks, keep packaging.

- Use included gauze to clean and dry area around chest wound.

- Grip red tab and peel off backing.

- Place dressing over chest wound, ensure wound is in the center of dressing.

- Smooth out edges of dressing ensuring good contact with skin.

- Repeat steps 3 – 7 for all chest wounds requiring dressings.

- Continually monitor for signs of a tension pneumothorax.

#### Contraindications

- Nil when indicated.

#### Complications

- Occlusion of the vents may lead to a tension pneumothorax.

#### Specific Notes

- If the patient develops a tension pneumothorax remove or ‘burp’ the chest seal and/or perform chest decompression.
